free_text: -ACTIVATION CROSS SECTIONS OF NEUTRON THRESHOLD
REACTIONS ON SOME ZIRCONIUM ISOTOPES IN THE 5.4- TO
10.6-MEV ENERGY RANGE.
VARIABLE ENERGY COMPACT CYCLOTRON CV28.
BY VARYING THE DEUTERON BEAM BETWEEN 3 AND
8 MEV QUASI-MONOENERGETIC NEUTRONS IN THE RANGE 5 TO
10.6 MEV WERE PRODUCED.
.HIGH PURITY NATURAL ZRO2 PACKED IN A POLYVINYL
CHLORIDE CAPSULE OF 1.1 CM DIAM, 2.6 CM LENGTH AND
0.1 CM WALL THICKNESS. THE AL OR FE 200 MICRON MONITOR
FOILS WERE ATTACHED AT THE FRONT AND BACK. ALSO A
20 MICRON AU FOIL WAS ATTACHED IN FRONT TO DETERMINE
THE THERMAL NEUTRON CONTRIBUTION. THE DISTANCE BETWEEN
GAS TARGET BEAM STOP AND FRONT OF SAMPLE WAS ABOUT
.MONITORED NEUTRON FLUX DENSITY AT THE SAMPLE POSITION
WAS BETWEEN 8.2E+6 AND 2.7E+7 N/CM2/SEC, THERMAL
NEUTRON FRACTION < 1.0 %.
.THE ACTIVITIES OF THE MONITOR PRODUCTS WERE CORRECTED
FOR CONTRIBUTIONS FROM BACKGROUND NEUTRONS (GAS-OUT/
GAS-IN RESULTS AND DEUTERON BREAKUP ON H-2 GAS).
.THE GAMMA DETECTOR COUNT RATES WERE CORRECTED FOR
SELF-ABSORPTION, PILEUP AND COINCIDENCE LOSSES. THE
BACKGROUND NEUTRON CONTRIBUTION WAS ALSO SUBTRACTED
AS FOR THE MONITOR REACTION PRODUCTS.
TOTAL ERROR, RANGE 10 - 12 %.
.DATA TAKEN FROM MAIN REFERENCE.
S.W.
S.W. SUBENTS 002 - 006 EACH SPLIT INTO TWO
SUBENTS, ONE FOR EACH MONITOR REACTION.
